Preprocess inputs for BRIERi (genotype as predictors)

preprocessI.Rd

Aligns external model coefficients to the SNP order of a target individual-level genotype matrix for use with BRIERi. target.info describes the columns of the target X matrix (in order) and defines the canonical allele orientation: external coefficients are matched by chromosome and position, then sign-flipped where their alleles are swapped relative to target.info.

Usage

preprocessI(
  target.info,
  external.ss = NULL,
  target.info.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),
  external.ss.cols = c(chr = "CHR", bp = "BP", ref = "REF", alt = "ALT"),
  external.coef.cols = NULL,
  drop.ambiguous = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

target.info

A data.frame describing the columns of the target genotype matrix in order. Must contain chromosome, basepair, REF, ALT.

external.ss

Optional data.frame of external model coefficients. Must contain chromosome, basepair, REF, ALT, plus one or more coefficient columns.

target.info.cols

Named character vector mapping chr, bp, ref, alt to column names in target.info.

external.ss.cols

Named character vector mapping chr, bp, ref, alt to column names in external.ss.

external.coef.cols

Character vector of column names in external.ss that hold model coefficients. Required when external.ss is supplied.

drop.ambiguous

Logical. If TRUE (default), drops strand-ambiguous SNPs (A/T, T/A, C/G, G/C alleles) from every input before matching.

verbose

Logical. If TRUE (default), prints SNP counts at each filter step.

Value

A list with the following elements:

target.info

Processed target SNP info as a data.frame with columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT. Rows correspond to the surviving columns of the target X matrix.

target.info.keep

Integer vector of indices into the original target.info for the surviving SNPs. Use as X[, target.info.keep] to subset the target genotype matrix to the analysis set.

external.ss

Processed external coefficients (or NULL if no external input was supplied, or if every supplied external model had all-zero coefficients after alignment), with columns varnames, CHR, BP, REF, ALT, coef1, coef2, ... where surviving coefficient columns are renumbered coef1, ..., coefK in input order.

external.coef.names

Named character vector mapping the standardized column names (coef1, coef2, ...) back to the original column names supplied in external.coef.cols, or NULL if no external input survived.

summary

Named integer vector of SNP counts: n.input.tinfo, n.input.ess, n.dropped.invalid.chr, n.dropped.ambiguous, n.dropped.multiallelic, n.final, n.external.matched, n.external.flipped, n.external.conflict, n.external.zero.

Details

BRIERi estimates an intercept internally from the target data, so external model intercepts are not required as input. The beta.external matrix passed to BRIERi must include an intercept slot as the first row (typically zero, since no external intercept is being imposed); see the example below.

Inputs are validated for missing values: if NA is detected in any of the required columns of target.info or external.ss (chr, bp, ref, alt, or coefficient columns), the function stops with an error. Clean the inputs before calling.

Chromosome coding is normalized internally: "chr" prefix is stripped, PLINK numeric codes (23/24/26) mapped to "X"/"Y"/"MT", and "M" normalized to "MT". SNPs with unrecognized CHR values (anything not in "1"\(-\)"22", "X", "Y", "MT", "XY") are dropped with a warning. varnames in the output uses the canonical form.

External models whose coefficient column is entirely zero after alignment (no overlap with target.info) are dropped from the output. If all supplied external models are dropped, the function emits a warning and returns external.ss = NULL.

Multi-allelic variants (duplicate CHR:BP entries) are dropped with a warning from every input independently. Strand-ambiguous SNPs (alleles A/T or C/G) are dropped by default; set drop.ambiguous = FALSE to keep them when strand is reliably matched across studies.

All three relevant data.frames (target.info, external.ss) share a varnames column of the form "CHR:BP:REF:ALT" (in the target.info orientation) as the first column.

See also

BRIERi, preprocessS, mergeExternals

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# target.info parallels the columns of X
out <- preprocessI(
  target.info        = target_info,
  external.ss        = prs_coefs,
  external.coef.cols = c("PRS_EUR", "PRS_EAS")
)

# Subset X to the surviving SNPs
X.aligned <- X[, out$target.info.keep]

# Extract beta.external matrix and prepend a zero row for the intercept
# slot expected by BRIERi (BRIERi estimates the intercept internally).
beta.ext <- as.matrix(
  out$external.ss[, grep("^coef", colnames(out$external.ss))]
)
beta.ext <- rbind(0, beta.ext)

fit <- BRIERi(
  X             = X.aligned,
  y             = y,
  beta.external = beta.ext,
  ...
)
} # }
